items.title: Almost 1.2 billion people living with mental disorders worldwide as case numbers nearly double since 1990
items.summary: A surge over the last three decades means mental disorders now account for more than 17 per cent of all disability worldwide, according to a report published this week.

items.title: What is 'pink slime' journalism and has it infiltrated Australian media?
items.summary: Experts say a series of AI-generated digital news outlets is the first recorded example of "pink slime" journalism in Australia, with the website owner claiming the now-deleted outlets were an experiment gone wrong.

items.title: NT politician Clinton Howe's former staffer lodges unfair dismissal application with Fair Work Commission
items.summary: A former staffer for Northern Territory government backbencher Clinton Howe is claiming she was sacked for refusing to falsify constituent information in a database, and was bullied and micromanaged by the politician's full-time electorate officer.

items.title: Sale's premier sporting export Scott Pendlebury almost never played AFL

items.title: Live: ASX to climb, oil drops on US–Iran deal hopes

items.title: Berlin's state parliament approves bid to rehost Olympics on or after 100th anniversary of Nazi-run Games

items.summary: The parents of two children in Northern Territory foster care have spoken out about their "heartbreaking" experience with the system, saying concerns their daughter was being abused by her foster family were brushed off by the department.

items.title: Sweeping cuts but little detail — can Tasmanian government stick to Treasurer Eric Abetz's budget?

items.title: One Nation surfs wave as Labor handles budget disquiet

items.title: Treasurer asked to explain productivity impact of tax changes
